canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, AI-generated text detection is most commonly defined as the task of classifying a given text as either human-written or machine-generated. This is frequently described as a "pressing need" to distinguish between the two sources ("BiScope: AI-generated Text Detection by Checking Memorization of Preceding Tokens"). While the dominant framing involves classifying an entire text, a more granular version of this behavior, termed `LLM text identification`, seeks to identify specific LLM-written segments within a mixed-origin document. A smaller set of studies investigates related but distinct tasks, such as `Self-written text recognition`, where a model discriminates its own writing, and `Copyrighted book detection`, a specific application for identifying excerpts from pretraining data. Another related task, `Model identification`, focuses on determining model lineage rather than the origin of a specific text. To operationalize this behavior, researchers employ various methods, which are broadly categorized as "training-based and training-free methods." Specific approaches mentioned for measurement include using Min-K% Prob, leveraging "conditional probability curvature" via tools like Fast-DetectGPT, or applying a "CREATIVITY INDEX" as a criterion for detection.
papers: AI as Humanity’s Salieri: Quantifying Linguistic Creativity of Language Models via Systematic Attribution of Machine Text against Web Text
CycleResearcher: Improving Automated Research via Automated Review
Deep Kernel Relative Test for Machine-generated Text Detection
Fine-tuning can Help Detect Pretraining Data from Large Language Models
Glimpse: Enabling White-Box Methods to Use Proprietary Models for Zero-Shot LLM-Generated Text Detection
Inspection and Control of Self-Generated-Text Recognition Ability in Llama3-8b-Instruct
PaLD: Detection of Text Partially Written by Large Language Models
REEF: Representation Encoding Fingerprints for Large Language Models
Training-free LLM-generated Text Detection by Mining Token Probability Sequences
BiScope: AI-generated Text Detection by Checking Memorization of Preceding Tokens
DPIC: Decoupling Prompt and Intrinsic Characteristics for LLM Generated Text Detection

definitions.text: The observable task of identifying which specific segments (e.g., sentences) within a mixed-origin text were generated by an LLM.

definitions.text: Classifying a text as machine-generated or human-written from its observed content.

definitions.text: The task of determining whether a suspect model is a subsequent development of a given victim model based on its intrinsic properties.
